key_insight: Bayesian inference on photoluminescence images plus drift-diffusion modeling can map where perovskite solar cells degrade and reveal whether the culprit is the bulk or specific interfaces.
engineering_relevance: This enables more targeted durability improvements by identifying the dominant failure locations and showing that amino-silane passivation can suppress interface-driven degradation.
method: Photoluminescence imaging of fully fabricated perovskite solar cells
Drift-diffusion simulations linked to measured optical data
Bayesian inference to generate spatial maps of recombination-related parameters over aging time
evidence_signal: The study tracks inferred parameter maps during aging at 70 °C under full-spectrum sunlight and finds pronounced spatially non-uniform degradation, strongest at perovskite/transport-layer interfaces.
watch_item: The inference depends on model assumptions and image quality, so parameter maps may be sensitive to simulation fidelity, prior choices, and experimental variability.
paper_title: Disentangling the origin of degradation in perovskite solar cells via optical imaging and Bayesian inference
